description:
## Goal

Transform the compiler and runtime from "plain `eprintln!` with stage-tagged string messages" to a coherent diagnostic system that produces output in the spirit of rustc / Elm:

```
warning: unreachable branch
  --> fixtures/modules.fz:12:7
   |
12 |   if x == "hello", do: ...
   |      ^^^^^^^^^^^^
   |
   = note: at this point, `x : int | atom(:ok)` â€” `x == "hello"` can never be true
   = help: did you mean `x == :hello`?
```

Beautiful, detailed, concise. Every error in every pipeline stage flows through the same infrastructure.

## Why now

The .11.24 typer-reintegration arc closed with structured `Diagnostic { fn_name, kind, message }` values in `src/ir_typer.rs:546-556` â€” but the `message` is built with `format!("â€¦bb{} v{}â€¦", b.id.0, v.0)`, which is IR vocabulary that means nothing to the user. The set-theoretic `Descr: Display` impl at `src/types.rs:745-768` is fully built (and currently dead-code) precisely to render types in source vocabulary. The missing infrastructure is *between* those two: source spans threaded through the pipeline so that "v3 narrowed to none" can become "`x` at fixtures/modules.fz:12:7, of type `int | atom(:ok)`, intersected with `str` is uninhabited."

## Current state (from investigation 2026-05-11)

### What carries position
- `Token { tok, line: u32, col: u32 }` (`src/lexer.rs:71-76`). No byte offset â€” accurate underlining (multi-byte UTF-8, tab expansion, unicode width) is impossible from `line:col` alone.
- `LexError`, `ParseError` carry `line, col` and pre-render to a string at the error site (`src/lexer.rs:92-96`, `src/parser.rs:115-119`).
- Everything else loses location at the parser/AST boundary.

### What does NOT carry position
- **All AST nodes.** `Expr`, `Pattern`, `BitField`, `Item`, `FnDef`, `FnClause`, `MatchClause`, `WithBinding`, `ModuleDef`, `Program` (`src/ast.rs`). The parser allocates the line/col on its way in but throws it away.
- **All IR nodes.** `Stmt`, `Prim`, `Term`, `Block`, `FnIr`, `Module` (`src/fz_ir.rs`). Likewise â€” once `ir_lower` consumes the AST, locations are gone.
- **All resolve/macro transformations.** `src/resolve.rs::rewrite_expr` and `src/macros.rs::expand_expr` build fresh AST nodes (`*e = new_e` at `macros.rs:256`); macro-generated nodes have *no* original-source position. They need a *lineage* (`expanded from MacroCall at <span>`), not just a span.
- **All IR Vars.** `ir_lower` keeps `env: HashMap<String, Var>` (`src/ir_lower.rs:113-114`) live for resolution but drops the map when lowering finishes. The mapping `Var(3) â†’ "x"` would be cheap to preserve and is required for "v3" â†’ "`x`".
- **Diagnostic struct.** `ir_typer::Diagnostic { fn_name, kind, message }` has no span field. Only one variant in `DiagKind`: `UnreachableArm`.

### Error sinks today (all single-line `eprintln!` calls in `src/main.rs`)
- Lex/parse: `main.rs:165, 212` (preformatted `lex error at L:C: â€¦` / `parse error at L:C: â€¦`)
- Resolve: `main.rs:169, 220` ("module resolution: <string>")
- Macros: `main.rs:172, 224` ("macro expansion: <string>")
- ir_lower: `main.rs:228` ("lower: <string>")
- ir_codegen: `main.rs:239` (also `panic!` for trampoline overflow, schema lookup at `ir_codegen.rs:113, 121`)
- ir_typer: `main.rs:243` (the structured `Diagnostic`, but rendered as plain `eprintln!("warning: {fn_name}: {message}")`)
- Runtime/interp: `main.rs:185` ("runtime error: <string>")
- Test runner: `test_runner.rs:97-104` (println per failure with `<msg>` line)
- REPL: `repl.rs:54, 72, 93, 100, 104, 107, 113, 124, 134, 209` (uniform `Outcome::Err(String)` shape)

### Sources we have to render from
- Single source string passed to `Lexer::new(src)`. No multi-file loading exists in the live build â€” but `test_runner.rs:58` does `format!("{}\n{}", PRELUDE, user_src)`, which shifts every user line number by the prelude line count. **Today this corrupts line numbers in test errors**. A diagnostic infrastructure must model this: file_id 0 = prelude, file_id 1 = user file, with byte offsets within each.

## Design (decided here; child tickets implement)

1. **Span representation:** `Span { file: FileId, start: u32, end: u32 }` over byte offsets. 12 bytes, `Copy`. Renderer computes line/col on demand from the source bytes (handles tabs, multi-byte chars, column width correctly).

2. **`SourceMap`** owns `Vec<SourceFile { name: String, bytes: Arc<str> }>`. `FileId(u32)` indexes it. Cheap line-offset cache built lazily for rendering. Shared by lexer (registers source files), AST/IR (carries spans), renderer (extracts snippets).

3. **AST spans via `Spanned<T>`** wrapper for the recursive types (`Expr`, `Pattern`) â€” `pub struct Spanned<T> { pub node: T, pub span: Span }`. For struct types with a fixed shape (`FnDef`, `FnClause`, `Item`, `MatchClause`, etc.) add a `span: Span` field directly. Avoids pollution of variant-matching code while still attaching spans where they matter.

4. **Macro/resolve lineage:** introduce `SpanOrigin { primary: Span, expansions: Vec<Span> }` carried by every AST node that resolve or macro expansion creates from a *Value* (not a token). The renderer shows "expanded from `<macro>` at fixtures/x.fz:L:C" alongside the primary span.

5. **IR side-tables.** Instead of bloating `Prim` / `Stmt` / `Term`, ir_lower emits parallel `Vec<Span>` indexed by `(BlockId, stmt_idx)` and `Vec<Span>` indexed by `Var` (the var's defining-site span). One canonical `Var â†’ source_name` table. Module-level `Vec<Span>` for fn definition sites. The IR types stay clean; spans live on the `Module` next to the schemas.

6. **`Diagnostic` redesign:** replace the current shape with
```rust
pub struct Diagnostic {
    pub severity: Severity,        // Error | Warning | Note | Help
    pub code: Option<DiagCode>,    // "type/unreachable", "lower/unbound", â€¦
    pub message: String,           // the headline
    pub primary: SpanLabel,        // span + short label like "this branch"
    pub secondaries: Vec<SpanLabel>,
    pub notes: Vec<String>,        // "note: â€¦"
    pub helps: Vec<String>,        // "help: â€¦"
}
```
`DiagCode` is a stable string (`"type/unreachable-arm"`, `"lower/unbound"`, etc.) for documentation cross-reference and machine output.

7. **Renderer.** Hand-rolled (~300 LOC), not a third-party dep. Reasons: (a) the diagnostic shape is custom (macro lineage, set-theoretic type rendering); (b) Cargo.toml is intentionally lean â€” `ariadne` and `codespan-reporting` are well-engineered but pull transitive deps; (c) the renderer needs to be modifiable for future fz-specific affordances (e.g. type-narrowing diagrams in `.14`/`.19` arcs). Color via env var with `NO_COLOR` honored. Tab handling: re-expand at fixed width for caret alignment. ASCII-only carets in v1, unicode in v2.

8. **Multi-error collection.** New `Diagnostics` accumulator. Every stage *can* report many; the driver decides whether to bail or continue. v1: each stage still returns `Result<_, Diagnostics>` and bails on first; the type accommodates multi-error so future tickets can flip to "collect all parse errors" without breaking the wire format.

## Non-goals (out of scope for the .20 arc)

- IDE / LSP integration. JSON output is a v2.
- Auto-fix / suggested edits (`help:` lines are text only).
- Source maps from macro expansion that survive across `quote`/`unquote` boundaries fully (we capture the macro-call span, not the position of code synthesized inside the macro body).
- Sophisticated unicode column-width handling (defer to v2; ASCII-clean fixtures keep v1 honest).
- Re-flowing runtime panics into diagnostics. Cranelift trampoline panics stay as panics for now (see .20.7 for what's in/out of the wiring pass).

## Children (sequenced)

- **.20.1** SourceMap + Span + lexer byte offsets â€” foundation, no AST/IR change.
- **.20.2** AST spans via `Spanned<T>` and per-struct span fields; parser populates.
- **.20.3** Resolve + macros: preserve spans + carry `SpanOrigin` lineage for synthesized nodes.
- **.20.4** ir_lower side-tables: `Var â†’ source_name`, per-Stmt/Term spans, per-fn spans.
- **.20.5** Diagnostic type + DiagCode taxonomy + Diagnostics accumulator.
- **.20.6** Renderer (snippets, carets, gutter, multi-span, lineage).
- **.20.7** Wire all existing error sites through the renderer (lex/parse/resolve/macros/lower/type/codegen + the runtime/interp message path).
- **.20.8** Set-theoretic type rendering in diagnostic messages (closes the loop with `Descr: Display` and `Var â†’ source_name`).

## Cross-references

- Closes the gap identified in fz-ul4.11.25's "should the printers be wired back up?" discussion.
- Touches but does not depend on the .11.* runtime work â€” diagnostic infra is orthogonal to GC / processes / SIMD.
- Anticipates fz-ul4.19 (processes): runtime errors from a process should carry pid + frame information when this infrastructure lands; out of scope here, but the `Diagnostic` shape is designed to extend.
title: [EPIC] Diagnostic infrastructure: beautiful, detailed, concise errors
